title: Callista humeana
taxonomy: Callista humeana is a species of saltwater clam in the family Veneridae. It is a type of marine bivalve mollusk. The classification of Callista humeana is as follows: Kingdom: Animalia, Phylum: Mollusca, Class: Bivalvia, Order: Veneroida, Family: Veneridae, Genus: Callista, Species: C. humeana. This classification indicates that Callista humeana belongs to the phylum Mollusca and the class Bivalvia, which are characteristic of bivalve mollusks. The species is a member of the family Veneridae, which includes various types of clams.

intro: Callista humeana, also known as Callista, is a flowering plant that belongs to the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for alleviating symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
